# Questions in Livestream 54

Live Stream #54: Amun-Re Dumps Amonet.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=283" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:43</a> | How should we understand the reports of "armies in the sky" in Josephus, Tacitus, and related sources? | He treated them as a portent image that fits biblical locust-and-army symbolism rather than as straightforward proof of a christophany. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=692" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:32</a> | When was the Torah written: Bronze Age or Iron Age? | He argued that the source documents go back to the Bronze Age, while editing and compilation belong to the Iron Age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=1012" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:52</a> | Do you know Christian historians who are experts on Rome and first-century Judea? | He recommended Craig Evans, William H. C. Frend, and John McRay as useful starting points. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=1164" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:24</a> | Should someone ditch Hebrew and Greek grammar books and just use Strong's with an interlinear? | No. He strongly preferred real grammars and modern lexicons, saying Strong's lacks nuance and ignores language development. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=1451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:11</a> | Other than Dune, what is your favorite science-fiction book? | He said he does not have one favorite book and tends to follow authors more than individual titles or series. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=1736" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:56</a> | In Daniel 3:25, is the better translation "god" or "gods," and should the figure be read as a christophany? | He said the wording is plural, "like a son of the gods," and the text allows a divine, christophanic, theophanic, or angelic reading without enough evidence to insist on one. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=2111" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:11</a> | How old was Isaac at the Aqedah, and when should biblical ages and numbers be read literally or idiomatically? | He leaned toward Isaac being a young teenager and said numbers must be judged by context rather than one rigid rule. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=2633" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:53</a> | Does each person of the Trinity having a will conflict with the oneness of God? | No. He said the persons are unified in divine essence and purpose, so distinct wills do not imply sinful conflict. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=2961" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:21</a> | Can viewers post questions before the livestream begins? | Yes. He encouraged pre-posting questions in the live chat once the livestream thumbnail is up, especially for complex topics. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=3239" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:59</a> | If you were making art, how would you depict angels? | He said it depends on the medium and the effect being aimed at, such as power, protection, or historical symbolism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=3630" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:30</a> | Are the Nag Hammadi texts representative of average Gnostic Christian belief, and are Gnostic ideas resurging? | He said Gnostic ideas never fully disappeared and that Nag Hammadi is a particular cache from a monastic setting, not a full cross-section of ordinary Gnostic belief. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=3997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:37</a> | Who are the angels in 2 Peter 2:4-7: watchers or other demonic beings? | He distinguished watchers from demons, saying 2 Peter is about fallen angels and that watchers are a different class of fallen spiritual beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=4445" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:05</a> | What are Messianic Jewish congregations like, including concerns about doctrine and lost-tribes ideas? | He described the movement as a broad tent that is still sorting out its identity, warned that some branches go in questionable directions, and praised New Testament-shaped Messianic Jewish congregations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=4863" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:03</a> | How would he steelman young-earth creationism against archaeological sites older than 6,000 years, such as Gobekli Tepe? | He said a strict 6,000-year chronology is hard to maintain archaeologically and that a stronger young-earth case would have to rely more on exegesis and scientific arguments than simplistic timeline harmonization. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=5242" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:22</a> | Do you know good books on the Hittites and Neo-Assyrians? | He recommended Harry Hoffner and Trevor Bryce for Hittite studies and mentioned Alan Millard, Grayson, and Lambert as useful Assyrian-related starting points. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=5555" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:35</a> | Is there more than one Satan in the Bible? | He said "satan" can function as a role or title for accusers, while there is still one preeminent Satan; Job, the New Testament, and Peter's rebuke illustrate the range. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=6143" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:23</a> | Have you considered a collaboration with World History by a Jew? | He said collaborations are possible when the fit is good, but his time is limited and he does not know that channel well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=6444" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:24</a> | Who or what was Azazel in Leviticus 16, and did that idea change by the Second Temple period? | He said the Leviticus context points first to the loss or removal of sin rather than a named evil being, though later interpretations personified or conflated Azazel more strongly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=6850" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:10</a> | Was Jesus born during Sukkot around 4 BCE? | He said it is difficult to know exactly and would not divide over it, though he personally leaned more toward a spring birth and thought 4 BCE was plausible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=7125" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:45</a> | How can someone tell whether a Bible translation is good? | He said the best check is knowledge of the original languages, but otherwise readers must rely on qualified evaluators who know both the source and target language well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=7430" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:50</a> | Does the Bible teach matrilineal descent, and is Ezra's expulsion of foreign wives evidence for it? | He said Ezra is about covenant contamination through foreign gods, while matrilineal descent becomes much clearer only in the later rabbinic period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=7817" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:17</a> | Should scholars abandon "Gnostic" as a heuristic label? | No. He said the category still has technical value because it names a real cluster of recurring beliefs even if the groups were diverse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=8324" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:44</a> | What is your view of abstract objects and essences, such as Platonic, Aristotelian, or nominalist views? | He said he does not take a strong position and would need someone more specialized in philosophy for a fuller answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=8503" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:43</a> | Before Christ, was Yahweh semi-deist, revealing himself only rarely except to Moses and a few prophets? | He rejected "semi-deist" as the right label, saying Yahweh is transcendent but still makes himself known through Israel, other prophets, and creation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=8850" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:30</a> | Is Tartarus basically synonymous with Gehenna? | He said the two images are not identical in origin, but in biblical context both point to the worst place of judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=9050" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:50</a> | How did Sabbath and Passover obligations apply to foreign slaves in Israel? | He stressed that everyone in the household, including slaves, was bound by Sabbath rest, while Passover participation was regulated by circumcision. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=9612" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:12</a> | Why was the nation of Israel chosen? | He answered from Deuteronomy: Israel was chosen because God loved the patriarchs and intended to work through their descendants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/FT2gTO3om8U?t=10007" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:47</a> | If the Torah was edited, what does that mean for biblical inerrancy? | He said it depends on what kind of editing is meant: content-changing redaction is a problem, but compilation, glosses, spelling updates, and inspired additions need not threaten inerrancy. |
